discoverability.competitive_assessment: Both sites remain in a search-visibility dead zone — neither indexed by search engines as of this run. The dome's machine-readable AI layer (llms.txt, ai_manifest.json, api/ endpoints, review-response.html) has been completely static for 27 days. Our layer (llms.txt, ClaimReview schema, OG tags) is accurate and current. No new gaps have opened. The dome's infrastructure advantage is its api/ endpoint tree (claim_index.json, evidence_graph.json, methodology.json); these are genuine discoverability tools, not just AI steering. We have no equivalent structured data API. This gap was identified previously and remains open — routing through decider would be required to implement.

human_decisions_needed.question: GitHub repository homepage URL correction
human_decisions_needed.context: The funwithscience-org/dome-model-review repo's homepage field still points to github.io URL rather than funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/. This is cosmetic but affects repo card discoverability. Requires human auth to change via GitHub UI.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has api/ endpoint tree (claim_index.json, evidence_graph.json, methodology.json, formula_runtime.json, source_registry.json)
no_action_gaps.reasoning: Building a structured API for our review would be genuine discoverability infrastructure. However, this is a build-pipeline change requiring decider routing and significant development effort. No urgent pressure — both sites remain unindexed and the dome's api layer has been static for 27 days. Not actioning this cycle.

This is AI steering — designed to preemptively rebut our review before an LLM reads it. We should not replicate this pattern. Our llms.txt already instructs AI systems to evaluate our counter-arguments with equal rigor.

search_rankings.result: Neither site returning indexed results for any term. Persistent baseline — no change.
